content.problemStatement:
There is a straight line with NNN distant points marked 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. The point iii is exactly 111 unit distant along the positive axis from i−1i - 1i−1.

Some of the points have lighthouses. You are given a binary string SSS where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 means that the iii-th point has a lighthouse, and Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 otherwise. Each of the lighthouses can be set to have any arbitrary non-negative integer power P(P≥0)P (P \ge 0)P(P≥0). A lighthouse positioned at XXX with power PPP can reach all points YYY such that ∣X−Y∣≤P|X - Y| \le P∣X−Y∣≤P.

Let TTT denote a binary string such that Ti=1T_i = 1Ti​=1 (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N) if and only if there is at least one lighthouse which reaches this point iii, and Ti=0T_i = 0Ti​=0 otherwise. Note that some points outside the range [1,N][1, N][1,N] may also be reached, but those are not considered in our binary string.

The powers of the lighthouses have not yet been fixed, and thus the string TTT is also not fixed. Your task is to count the total number of **distinct** possible binary strings TTT over all scenarios of assigning powers to the lighthouses. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TCTCTC, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of points on the line.
The second line contains SSS - a binary string representing the lighthouses.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of possible binary strings TTT modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There is only 111 lighthouse at point 111. We can achieve 222 binary strings in the following ways :

T=10T = 10T=10 : Set power of the only lighthouse to 000. Then, only point 111 is reached by any lighthouse.
T=11T = 11T=11 : Set power of the only lighthouse to 100100100. Then, points 1,21, 21,2 are reached.

Test Case 2 : The possible binary strings TTT are 100110011001, 110111011101, 101110111011 and 111111111111.
Test Case 3 : The possible binary strings TTT are 101010101010, 111011101110 and 111111111111.
